workflow.name: Enrich company leads and find HR contacts with Google Sheets, OpenAI and LinkFinder AI
workflow.description:
## Description

This workflow is **Part 2** of the HR Client Acquisition system and builds on the lead discovery pipeline from the previous workflow:

🔗 [HR Client Acquisition (Part 1) – Job Lead Discovery & AI Qualification System](https://n8n.io/workflows/14859-scrape-and-qualify-hr-job-leads-with-apify-google-sheets-and-openai-gpt-4o-mini/)

In Part 1, job leads are discovered and qualified using AI.  
In this workflow (Part 2), those qualified companies are enriched further by identifying company domains, classifying them as employers or agencies, and extracting decision-maker contacts such as HR and operations leaders.

The workflow uses AI, web scraping, and enrichment APIs to transform raw company data into actionable outreach-ready leads with verified contact information.

## Use cases

- Recruitment agencies targeting companies actively hiring  
- Sales teams building high-quality outbound lead lists  
- Automating employer research and contact discovery  
- Enriching job-based leads into decision-maker pipelines  

## Requirements

- Google Sheets account  
- OpenAI API key  
- Apify account  
- LinkFinder AI API key  
- n8n instance with environment variables configured  

## How to use

1. Run Part 1 workflow to collect and qualify job leads  
2. Ensure companies are stored in Google Sheets with status = NEW or ENRICHMENT_REQUIRED  
3. Execute this workflow  
4. Workflow enriches companies and extracts key contacts  
5. Contacts are saved into Google Sheets for outreach  

## Customising this workflow

- Modify AI prompts for company classification  
- Adjust industry filtering logic  
- Change contact selection priorities (HR, operations, leadership)  
- Integrate additional enrichment or CRM tools  

## What this template demonstrates

- Multi-step lead enrichment pipeline  
- AI-powered company classification  
- Domain extraction and validation  
- Contact discovery using external APIs  
- Structured data handling using Google Sheets  

## How it works

• Step 1: Fetch qualified companies from Google Sheets  
• Step 2: Extract or predict company domain  
• Step 3: Scrape and analyze website content using AI  
• Step 4: Classify company as employer or agency  
• Step 5: Enrich employer data and fetch employee contacts  
• Step 6: Select top contacts and store results  

## Setup steps

• Estimated setup time: 15–25 minutes  
• Configure API keys (OpenAI, Apify, LinkFinder)  
• Set environment variables for Google Sheets  
• Connect output of Part 1 workflow  
• Run test execution with sample data

workflow.name: Enrich company leads and extract decision-maker contacts using Linkfinder AI

parameters.content:
## Company Enrichment Lead Generation

### How it works

The workflow fetches data from Google Sheets to identify new status entries.
It extracts domains from existing data or utilizes AI to process domain information.
A web scraping module scrapes websites to gather HTML content.
AI agencies process the scraped content to classify them as an employer or agency.
Identified employers undergo further enrichment through API calls and saved back to Google Sheets.
Contacts information is processed, emails and LinkedIn profiles are amended, and results are stored.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Set up Google Sheets API credentials for access to existing spreadsheets.
- [ ] Configure OpenAI API credentials for AI processing nodes.
- [ ] Ensure Apify and LinkFinder AI API keys are configured for data scraping nodes.
- [ ] Customize domain extraction logic to suit specific data patterns.

## Workflow trigger and initialization

Initial trigger to start the workflow, fetching initial data from Google Sheets.

## Filter new status

Filter rows to find entries with a new status for processing.

## Batch and read companies

Split data into batches and read existing companies from the sheet.

## Domain handling process

Decision-making for domain existence and extraction from email; AI domain prediction if needed.

## Website scraping

Scrape website based on processed domain and evaluate website content with AI.

## AI classification and action

Classify content using AI and determine actions (e.g., enrich with data or skip).

## Mark entities

Mark entries as required or not for enrichment within Google Sheets.

## Employer process branching

Decide on further processing based on employer classification (e.g., API actions, contact enrichment).

## AI and contact data merge

Execute AI to select top contacts, parse the result, and handle missing information.

## HR employee scraping

Use API calls to gather HR employee data from an external source.

## Employer enrichment

Run external data actor and process the dataset for potential employer data enrichment.

## Contact processing and saving

Parse selected contact information and save enriched results to Google Sheets.

## LinkedIn processing

Evaluate LinkedIn presence and scrape profile data if available.

## Agency marking

Mark entries as agency-related within Google Sheets.

values.skipSelectors: script, style, noscript, svg, img, header, footer, nav

values.content:
=Here is your **same prompt with ONLY the industry classification added (no other changes):**

---

You are a company classifier.

Determine if this company is a REAL EMPLOYER or a RECRUITMENT/STAFFING AGENCY.

Company name: {{ $node["🌐 AI Domain Found?"].json.company_name }}

Website URL: {{ $node["Save Domain to Companies"].json.company_website }}

Website content (first 3000 chars): {{
($json.clean_content && $json.clean_content.length > 0
? $json.clean_content
: $json.data?.markdown || ''
).slice(0, 3000)
}}

CLASSIFICATION RULES:

* Staffing/recruitment/manpower supply/HR outsourcing/placing candidates → AGENCY
* Job board, job aggregator, employment portal → AGENCY
* "We provide manpower", "recruitment solutions", "staffing services" → AGENCY
* Real operating business (hotel, hospital, logistics, construction, oil company, restaurant, factory) → EMPLOYER
* Unclear or website unavailable → EMPLOYER (benefit of doubt)

INDUSTRY CLASSIFICATION:
Classify the company into exactly ONE of these industries:

* Oil & Gas
* Construction
* Services (Banking & Insurance)
* Logistics & Supply Chain
* Manufacturing
* Hospitality
* Telecommunications & IT
* Facility Management
* Aviation / Airport
* Retail
* Shipbuilding
* Automobile
* Drivers & Operators
* Furniture & Interiors
* Garments & Furnishing
* Agriculture
* Community Services
* Education
* Healthcare
* Other

RETURN ONLY VALID JSON:

```json
{
  "classification": "EMPLOYER",
  "industry": "Hospitality",
  "reason": "one sentence explanation"
}
```

=You are an outbound targeting engine for a manpower recruitment agency.

Your goal is to ALWAYS return 1–2 contacts WITH VALID EMAILS ONLY.

-------------------------------------
CONTEXT:
We supply manpower (hospitality, logistics, healthcare, oil & gas, construction).
We help companies fill urgent hiring needs with international candidates.

EMPLOYEE DATA:
{{ JSON.stringify($input.all().map(item => item.json)) }}

JOB CONTEXT:
- Job Role: {{ $('🔄 Loop: Each Job2').item.json.job_title }}
- Job Location: {{ $('🔄 Loop: Each Job2').item.json.job_location }}
- Company: {{ $('🔄 Loop: Each Job2').item.json.company_name }}

-------------------------------------
STEP 1: HARD FILTER (EMAIL REQUIRED)

ONLY keep contacts where:
- Email EXISTS and is valid

REMOVE ALL contacts without email immediately.

-------------------------------------
STEP 2: ROLE FILTER (FLEXIBLE)

Prefer roles in this order:

1. Recruiter / Talent Acquisition
2. HR Manager / HRBP
3. Operations Manager / Operations roles
4. ANY senior-level role (Director, VP, Head, Manager)

👉 If ideal roles are not available:
→ ALLOW less-perfect but still senior roles WITH email

-------------------------------------
STEP 3: COMPANY MATCH

- MUST match same company (or very close variation like Weatherford vs Weatherford International)

-------------------------------------
STEP 4: FINAL SELECTION

- Select MAXIMUM 2 contacts
- MUST return at least 1 if any email exists
- If multiple valid → pick best 2 by relevance

- If NO contacts with email exist:
  → return empty array

-------------------------------------
OUTPUT RULES

- DO NOT hallucinate emails
- DO NOT include contacts without email
- Output STRICT JSON only

-------------------------------------
OUTPUT FORMAT:

{
  "total_selected": 2,
  "selected_contacts": [
    {
      "name": "John Doe",
      "role": "Senior Recruiter",
      "email": "john@company.com",
      "linkedinUrl": "https://linkedin.com/in/johndoe",
      "companylinkedinUrl": "https://linkedin.com/company/company-name",
      "location_match": true,
      "needs_enrichment": false,
      "priority": "HIGH",
      "reason": "Relevant role with confirmed email"
    }
  ],
  "strategy": "Prioritize contacts with email for immediate outreach"
}

user.name: Dinakar Selvakumar
user.bio: Turning real business problems into clean n8n automations. I design reliable templates for social media, lead generation, AI agents, and internal workflows, with a strong focus on simplicity, security, and great user experience.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.

data.details: The Code node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow.

The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
